244	- **`Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` + `exact_mod_cast _hz` is the 2-LOC canonical closure for `ringKrullDim _ = 1` on codim-1 stalks** *(NEW iter-184 Archon canonical — Lane M↓ `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` `hreg_dim` Krull-dim half.)* When the goal is `ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1` and `_hz : Order.coheight z = 1` is in scope, the closure is `rw [Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight]; exact_mod_cast _hz`. **First consumer** of the iter-183 axiom-clean `Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean` bridge. `exact_mod_cast` bridges `Order.coheight z : ℕ∞` to `ringKrullDim _ : WithBot ℕ∞` via the `(1 : ℕ∞) ↦ (1 : WithBot ℕ∞)` coercion — matching the pattern in `CoheightBridge.ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one` (CoheightBridge L228-233). Requires `import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.CoheightBridge`. Reusable for any "Krull-dim of a stalk equals codim-coheight" obligation downstream of the CoheightBridge instance.
245	
246	- **`@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` `pullback.lift_fst/_snd _ _ _` wrappers fill the Mathlib `@[simp]`-on-pullback.map projection gap** *(NEW iter-184 Archon canonical — Lane B `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean` Recipe 1 helpers `pullback_map_fst_proj` / `pullback_map_snd_proj`, both Tier-1 axiom-clean and globally active across the project.)* Mathlib ships `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` as `@[reassoc]` ONLY, NOT `@[simp]`, so `simp` does not collapse `pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫ pullback.fst g₁ g₂` even though the equation `pullback.fst f₁ f₂ ≫ i₁` holds definitionally. The fix is a 2-helper-per-file (or upstream-Mathlib-PR) shim:
247	  ```lean
248	  @[reassoc (attr := simp)]
249	  lemma pullback_map_fst_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
250	      (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
251	      (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
252	      (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
253	      Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫ Limits.pullback.fst g₁ g₂ =
254	        Limits.pullback.fst f₁ f₂ ≫ i₁ :=
255	    Limits.pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
256	  ```
257	  (mirror `_snd_proj` similarly). Globally active once added at file top. **Reusable for any project that consumes `asIso (pullback.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ eq₁ eq₂)`-style cover-intersection isos** (e.g. Proj-of-MvPolynomial chart covers). **Upstream candidate**: file an upstream Mathlib PR to upgrade `pullback.lift_fst` / `_snd` to `@[simp]` (the iter-184 `gmscaling-projection-idiom` analogist consult diagnosed this as a Mathlib infelicity, not a project-design issue).
258	
259	- **`@Order.coheight … (specializationPreorder …)` explicit pinning is required when stating coheight/height lemmas on topological spaces** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical — `Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding` in `Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean`, all 4 declarations Tier-1 axiom-clean.)* When `Order.coheight` is used on a `TopologicalSpace X` that lacks an automatic `[Preorder X]` derivation, the statement type must use explicit `@`-syntax to pin the `Preorder` instance to `specializationPreorder` (because `specializationPreorder` is marked `@[instance_reducible]` but is NOT a true typeclass instance), AND the `Subtype.preorder` global instance — which derives `Preorder ↥U` from `Preorder X` automatically — conflicts with `specializationPreorder ↥U` on the subspace topology. Workaround: state the lemma with `@Order.coheight α (specializationPreorder α)` on BOTH sides; bind `letI : Preorder X := specializationPreorder X` and same for `↥U` INSIDE the proof body. Reusable for any coheight/height-on-topological-space declaration. **Companion**: for the algebra-side bridge (`ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight`), `IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`'s `[Algebra R A]` argument does NOT auto-synthesize even when `TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk` is registered as an instance; explicit `letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk _ _` required.
260	
261	- **`induction n generalizing M` is load-bearing for the Stacks 00LP depth-via-Ext inductive chase** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical — `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` partial proof in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`; base case + backward Steps 1-2 closed kernel-clean.)* When inducting on `n : ℕ` for a depth-via-Ext claim of shape `(n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M ↔ ∀ i < n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0`, the `generalizing M` clause is essential — the step-case IH must be universally quantified over the module so it can recurse onto `M/xM = QuotSMulTop x M`, NOT just `M`. The companion Tier-1 axiom-clean helper is `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator : x ∈ Module.annihilator R N → x • e = 0 for e : Ext^i(N, M)` proven via the R-linearity chain `(mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e _ = x • e` (using `mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp`) + `x • 𝟙_N = 0` + `mk₀ 0 = 0` + `zero_comp`. The precise statement of the Stacks 00LP "x annihilates Ext^*(κ, ·)" trick, generalised to arbitrary `x ∈ Ann(N)` so it also covers `N = R/(x_1, …, x_k)`. **Tooling trap**: `private lemma h𝟙 :` fails to parse — `𝟙` is reserved in some contexts; rename. `Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul` needs explicit `(R := R)` for TC even when `R` is in scope.
262	
263	- **`IsOpenImmersion.lift` is the right factorisation hook when the substantive content is range containment** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical — `iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`; structural reduction iter-183, range containment ~10-15 LOC iter-184.)* When a factorisation lemma `∃ r : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec(Away 𝒜 (X i)), q ≫ Proj.awayι (X i) _ _ = onePt.left` is needed and a direct construction via `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom ∘ eval ∘ chartIso)` requires a substantive chase via `fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`, the Mathlib idiom `IsOpenImmersion.lift (Proj.awayι (X i) _ _) onePt.left ?_` collapses the residual obligation to just the range containment `Set.range onePt.left ⊆ Set.range (awayι (X i))`. The factorisation equation `r ≫ awayι = onePt.left` then comes from `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _` automatically. Located at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/OpenImmersion.lean:636-641`. Reusable for any "factor through an open immersion" obligation where the range containment is the natural geometric witness.
264	
265	- **`@[simps! hom left]` on `OverClass.asOver` is a trap for `rw [Over.w]` tactics — use term-mode `congrArg` workaround** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical — `iotaGm_inner_lift_compat` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, axiom-clean via the workaround.)* When the goal contains `(asOver X S).hom` (an `Over.OverMorphism` constructed via `OverClass.asOver`) and the proof needs `Over.w` to commute the structure morphism with `f.hom`, BOTH `rw [Over.w f]` AND `simp [Over.w]` FAIL. The cause: `@[simps! hom left]` on `OverClass.asOver` generates a simp lemma `asOver_hom : (asOver X S).hom = X ↘ S` that elaboration applies eagerly, replacing `(asOver X S).hom` with `X ↘ S` BEFORE the `Over.w` rewrite can fire. Fix: term-mode `congrArg ((g ≫ ·) (Over.w _))` (which bypasses simp-eager normalisation) OR `aesop_cat` as fallback. **Companion trap**: `rw [hfact]` on `hfact : f ≫ g = h` can fail when the goal contains `f ≫ g` (same elaboration mismatch family); workaround `have := lemma_using_h; rwa [← hfact] at this` (reverse rewrite on the produced fact). Reusable for any `Over.OverMorphism` proof involving the `OverClass.asOver` chain.
266	
267	- **`HasColimit (relativeGluingData _).functor` does NOT auto-synthesize through `abbrev`; explicit `haveI : ... .IsLocallyDirected` required** *(NEW iter-183 Archon canonical — `pullback_iso_construction` in `Picard/RelativeSpec.lean`; structured 5-helper proof.)* When directly invoking `colimit.desc (relativeGluingData _).functor _` for a `Spec`-of-quasi-coherent-algebra construction, the `HasColimit` instance does NOT auto-synthesize even though `T.RelativeSpec(𝒜) = (relativeGluingData _).glued = colimit ...` works as an `abbrev`. The fix: explicit `haveI : ((relativeGluingData _).functor ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected := Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _` to unblock the locally-directed property; `HasColimit` then synthesises. **Companion lemmas located**: `IsAffineOpen.SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec` (AffineScheme.lean:464); `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` (:451); `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι` (RelativeGluing.lean:166). Reusable for any project-side construction that directly invokes `colimit.desc` on a `RelativeGluingData.functor`-style diagram.
268	
269	- **Mathlib ships `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` at `AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:258` — the inner intersection iso `pullback awayι_i awayι_j ≅ Spec(Away(X_i X_j))`; the 4-step chain `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso ≪≫ pullback.congrHom condition rfl ≪≫ asIso (pullback.map _ _ _ _ _ _ pullbackAwayιIso.hom _) ≪≫ pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso` is the canonical bridge for cover-intersection identification** *(NEW iter-182 Archon canonical — `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` PROVEN axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, ~67 LOC.)* When the goal is to identify `pullback ((cover).f i) ((cover).f j)` with `Spec(Away(X_i * X_j))` in a `Proj`-of-MvPolynomial chart cover, do NOT build a project-side `Away_Xi_Xj_iso` — the iter-181 task_result that proposed this is WRONG. Use the 4-step Mathlib chain plus companion projection lemmas `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_{left,right}` and `Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι`. **GOTCHA — cocycle body still gated**: the iso itself lands kernel-clean, but the consumer `cancel_epi iso.inv` followed by `simp` on the cocycle body fails because the 7-stage iso chain expands to ~800-line composition that `simp` cannot collapse. iter-183 fix: `@[simps]` annotation OR 2 manual `_inv_comp_fst` / `_inv_comp_snd` projection lemmas (~30-40 LOC each) BEFORE the cocycle body can close. Reusable for any cover-intersection iso construction over `Proj` of MvPolynomial.
270	
271	- **`LES of Ext^*(κ, -)` via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` + `ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact` + 3-branch case split + `ENat` tsub bridges is the canonical recipe for SES depth-bound chase (Stacks 00LE)** *(NEW iter-182 Archon canonical — `RingTheory.Module.depth_of_short_exact` PROVEN Tier-2 (kernel-clean modulo upstream `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` typed sorry).)* For SES `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` in `ModuleCat R`, the recipe: (1) package as `ShortComplex.ShortExact` via `ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact`; (2) reduce `(↑a ≤ depth M)` via `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`; (3) per `a : ℕ`, expand `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` to chase Ext-vanishing through the LES at `κ = ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)`. **3-branch case split**: (3a) `min(depth N', depth N'') ≤ depth N` via postcomp `e ↦ e ∘ S.g` + exact₂; (3b) `min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ≤ depth N''` via postcomp by extClass + exact₃ + `natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one` helper; (3c) `min(depth N, depth N'' + 1) ≤ depth N'` case-split on `i = 0` (`postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono` for `S.f` mono) vs `i = j+1` (exact₁ lift at `n₀ = j, n₁ = j+1`). 2 reusable private helpers: `ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth` (Tier-2 modulo same upstream) + `natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one` (Tier-1 axiom-clean). **Tooling traps**: `simpa using h0` for the degree-0 mono case times out at 200000 heartbeats (`whnf` can't reduce `(postcomp _) e = (postcomp _) 0`) — workaround `apply hinj; simpa using hef` splits before normalisation. `Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero` returns `i = j.succ` not `i = j + 1` — extract predecessor + `omega` arithmetic. `one_ne_top` does not exist in scope; use `(by norm_num : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤)`. Reusable for any project-side LES-of-Ext chase in the depth layer; helpers promotable to public file namespace for downstream consumers (`auslander_buchsbaum_formula`, Cohen-Macaulay corollaries).
272	
273	- **Strictly-stronger named helper + `IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range` is the right refactor shape for `IsOpen (Set.range _)` goals** *(NEW iter-182 Archon canonical — `iotaGm_range_isOpen` body now 2 LOC via the new helper `iotaGm_isOpenImmersion`; analogist Decision 4 of `intersection-ring-cross01.md`.)* When the goal is `IsOpen (Set.range f)` for a composed morphism `f = g ≫ h` of schemes, the substantive content is almost always "the composition is an open immersion" — promote to a strictly-stronger named helper `helper : IsOpenImmersion (g ≫ h) := sorry`, then close the original via `haveI := helper ...; exact IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range _`. The helper carries the substantive content; the original is 2 lines. Bonus: downstream consumers gain the stronger conclusion for free (e.g. `iotaGm_isDominant` becomes `IsOpenImmersion + IrreducibleSpace` instead of `IsOpen + Nonempty`). **Dead end**: `exact IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range _` directly fails because typeclass synthesis cannot resolve the `IsOpenImmersion` instance on a composed morphism without unfolding. **Tooling trap**: `rw [Over.lift_left, Over.comp_left, ...]` in one tactic fails "motive is not type correct" because `Over.Hom.w` proof terms depend on rewritten expressions — use `simp only` (handles motive via congr lemmas) instead. Reusable for any "IsOpen of range of composed morphism" goal where the composition is mathematically an open immersion.
274	
275	- **`unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun; change ((ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom _)).appTop` is the prep that unblocks `rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]` in functor-Yoneda `right_inv` round-trips** *(NEW iter-181 Archon canonical — `gmHomEquiv_right_inv` body PROVEN kernel-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`; sealed the iter-180 BLOCKER comment that flagged the rewrite as failing to find the pattern.)* When `rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]` reports "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" on a `right_inv : toFun (invFun u) = u` goal whose `invFun` is `T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom <lift>)` wrapped inside an `(Over.homMk _ _).left.appTop`, the rewrite pattern `(Spec.map ?f)` cannot unify against the wrapped form. The fix: `unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun` then `change ((ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom _)).appTop` to syntactically expose the `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom _)` form, then `rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc, Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]` collapses LHS to `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap`, and `simp [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']` finishes. Reusable for any `Equiv.right_inv` proof on a Yoneda-style construction where `invFun` produces a `Spec.map` composition wrapped in an `Over.homMk` / `Over.OverMorphism`. **Companion idiom**: the `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` recipe (iter-180 KB entry) applies here too if the unfold+change still leaves `Algebra.compHom`-chain instance unification.
276	
277	- **`fin_cases` non-canonical Fin literal trap + `simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.zero_eta, Fin.mk_one]` pre-rewrite cleanup** *(NEW iter-181 Archon canonical — `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement` diagonal + (1,0)-from-(0,1) by `pullbackSymmetry`, axiom-clean.)* `fin_cases x` on `x : Fin n` produces `(fun i ↦ i) ⟨k, _⟩`, not the canonical `(k : Fin n)` literal that Mathlib lemmas like `pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_{fst,snd}` and the chart-bridge `Proj.awayι (![X 0, X 1] i)` pattern-match against. The fix: insert `simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.zero_eta, Fin.mk_one]` BEFORE the `rw` chain to canonicalise the Fin literal. Variant of the iter-180 Lane A `respectTransparency` recipe trap (same family: `Matrix.cons`/`Fin.cases` reduction surfacing in a different shape). Reusable for any `fin_cases`-driven goal where subsequent `rw`/`simp` fails on "pattern verbatim but not found" symptom.
278	
279	- **`pullbackSymmetry` epi argument derives `(1,0)`-cross-case from `(0,1)`-cross-case axiom-clean (no substantive content duplicated)** *(NEW iter-181 Archon canonical — `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement` (1,0) leg.)* When a cocycle's cross cases come in symmetric pairs (e.g. `chart 1 ≫ X 0 = chart 0 ≫ X 1` vs `chart 0 ≫ X 1 = chart 1 ≫ X 0`), do NOT re-prove both sides. The fix: pre-compose BOTH sides of the (1,0) goal with `(pullbackSymmetry _ _).hom : pullback (cover.f 1) (cover.f 0) ⟶ pullback (cover.f 0) (cover.f 1)` (an iso hence epi), apply `pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst` / `pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_snd` to swap the fst/snd legs, then close via `h01.symm` (the proven (0,1) leg). ~5 LOC. Reusable for any pullback-cocycle proof where the cross cases form a symmetric pair under `pullbackSymmetry`.
280	
281	- **`GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton + IsOpen.dense` derives `DenseRange` from `IsOpen + Nonempty` on a geometrically irreducible scheme over `Spec k̄`** *(NEW iter-181 Archon canonical — `iotaGm_isDominant` body PROVEN kernel-clean modulo upstream `iotaGm_range_isOpen` helper.)* When the goal is `IsDominant f` for `f : X ⟶ Y` over `Spec k̄` and `Y` carries the `[GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]` class, the recipe is: (1) reduce `IsDominant` → `DenseRange (f.left).base.app` via `Over.comp_left` + `IsDominant` definition; (2) `haveI : Subsingleton ↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := inferInstance` (auto from `PrimeSpectrum.instUnique` on a field); (3) `haveI hirr : IrreducibleSpace Y.left := GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (f := Y.hom)` (Mathlib `Geometrically/Irreducible.lean:99`); (4) `IrreducibleSpace` extends `PreirreducibleSpace`, so `IsOpen.dense` (Mathlib `Topology/Irreducible.lean`) fires on the open range; (5) `exact (range_isOpen.dense (Set.range_nonempty _))`. ~5 LOC plus a per-call `IsOpen (Set.range _)` helper. Reusable for any `IsDominant` obligation onto a geometrically irreducible target over `Spec k̄`.

655	### Sub-task (b): `zero_mem'` (line 263 — RESOLVED axiom-clean)
656	
657	```lean
658	refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩
659	· simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
660	· simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
661	```
662	
663	`Scheme.RationalMap.order Q 0 = WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 0) =
664	WithZero.log 0 = 0` is delivered by simp via `map_zero` and
665	`WithZero.log_zero`. Both `0 ≤ 0` and `-1 ≤ 0` are then trivial.

667	### Sub-task (c): `add_mem'` (line 268 — RESOLVED axiom-clean)
668	
669	The non-archimedean inequality on the discrete valuation of a
670	regular-in-codim-1 stalk (`Ring.ordFrac_add`, requires
671	`IsDiscreteValuationRing` on the stalk — supplied via iter-186 Step 1
672	class upgrade `IsRegularInCodimensionOne`).
673	
674	Proof structure:
675	1. Helper `hMNZ`: `Ring.ordFrac _ y ≠ 0 ↔ y ≠ 0` (via
676	   `MonoidWithZeroHom.map_eq_zero_iff`, available on a field source).
677	2. Per-Q helper `key`: for any prime divisor `Q` and integer `n ≤ 0`,
678	   `n ≤ order Q a` and `n ≤ order Q b` ⟹ `n ≤ order Q (a + b)`.
679	3. Case-split `a + b = 0` (trivial), `a = 0` (reduce to `order Q b`),
680	   `b = 0` (reduce to `order Q a`), else use `Ring.ordFrac_add` +
681	   `WithZero.log_le_log` monotonicity + `min_cases` to split the
682	   `min` into a concrete branch.
683	4. Apply `key` with `n = 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `n = -1` for `Q = P`.
684	
685	### Sub-task (d): `smul_mem'` (line 295 — RESOLVED axiom-clean)
686	
687	The `kbar`-scalar action `c • x = (algebraMap kbar K(C) c) * x` for any
688	`c, x` (via `Algebra.smul_def`). For `c ≠ 0` and `x ≠ 0`, the order
689	calculation reduces to `order Q (c • x) = order Q (algebraMap c) +
690	order Q x` (by multiplicativity + `WithZero.log_mul`).
691	
692	The non-trivial step: showing `order Q (algebraMap kbar K(C) c) ≥ 0`
693	for `c ≠ 0`. The lift `β := germ_at_Q (kToSection c) ∈ stalk_Q` is
694	nonzero (since `algebraMap c ≠ 0` and `α = algebraMap stalk K(C) β`
695	via `germ_stalkSpecializes_apply` functoriality). Then
696	`Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero` on `β` gives `1 ≤ ordFrac (algebraMap
697	stalk K(C) β) = ordFrac α`. Applying `WithZero.log_le_log` (with
698	`(1 : WithZero (Multiplicative ℤ)).log = 0`) gives `0 ≤ order Q α`.
699	
700	Combined with `n ≤ order Q x` (where `n ∈ {0, -1}`), `linarith` closes
701	`n ≤ order Q α + order Q x = order Q (c • x)`.
702	
703	Two-case-split before the meat: `c = 0` and `x = 0` reduce to
704	`zero_mem'`-style trivialities.

100	
101	In this project, a "`k`-group scheme" is encoded as an
102	`Over (Spec (.of k))`-object `G` carrying a `[GrpObj G]` instance (the
103	group-object structure in the over-category). The locally-of-finite-type
104	hypothesis is the `[LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]` instance on the structural
105	morphism to the base.
106	
107	Blueprint references: `def:identity_component_group_scheme` +
108	`thm:identity_component_open_subgroup` (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`). -/
109	
110	namespace GroupScheme
111	
112	/-- Helper (iter-186): the **clopen carrier `Set`** of the identity
113	component of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type, packaged as a
114	`G.left.Opens`. The intended substantive identity is: this is the (Zariski-)
115	open subset of `|G|` whose underlying set is the connected component of `|G|`
116	through the image of the identity section `e : Spec k → G`.
117	
118	The body is a *typed* `sorry` carrying Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3)
119	substantive content:
120	
121	* the carrier set is `connectedComponent x` for `x = e(*)` the image of
122	  the identity section (well-defined because `Spec k` is a singleton);
123	* openness is EGA I 6.1.9 — connected components of a locally Noetherian
124	  topological space are open. The relevant Mathlib hook is
125	  `isOpen_connectedComponent` (which needs a `LocallyConnectedSpace`
126	  instance on `G.left.toTopCat`; the implication "locally Noetherian
127	  topological space ⟹ locally connected" is a Mathlib gap at the current
128	  pinned commit and is the substantive content of this helper).
129	
130	Downstream consumers (`IdentityComponent`, `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`)
131	plug this typed sorry into their bodies and propagate the typed-sorry
132	discharge cleanly. -/
133	private noncomputable def identityComponentCarrier {k : Type u} [Field k]
134	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
135	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
136	    G.left.Opens :=
137	  sorry
138	
139	/-- The **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
140	finite type.
141	
142	Encoded as a `k`-scheme: an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`, carrying the
143	intended substantive identity "this is the connected component of `|G|`
144	containing the identity section `e`, equipped with the open-subscheme
145	structure inherited from `G`". The associated open immersion
146	`IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` is the content of
147	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`.
148	
149	iter-186 body: built from the `identityComponentCarrier G` helper — the
150	open subscheme of `G` whose underlying topological space is the connected
151	component of `|G|` through the image of the identity section. The
152	structure morphism is the inherited `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`.
153	The substantive content (the actual carrier `Set`, plus its openness from
154	EGA I 6.1.9: locally Noetherian spaces have open connected components)
155	lives in the typed-sorry body of `identityComponentCarrier`. -/
156	noncomputable def IdentityComponent {k : Type u} [Field k]
157	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
159	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
160	  Over.mk ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
161	
162	/-- **The identity component is an open and closed subgroup scheme.**
163	
164	The bundled statement of Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3): the identity
165	component `G^0 = IdentityComponent G` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
166	finite type comes with a morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` (in
167	`Over (Spec (.of k))`) whose underlying scheme morphism is both an open
168	immersion and a closed immersion (i.e. the inclusion of a clopen
169	subscheme).
170	
171	The full Kleiman conclusion also packages the group-subscheme property (the
172	inclusion is a homomorphism of `k`-group schemes), finite-type-ness over `k`
173	(`LocallyOfFiniteType` + quasi-compactness), geometric irreducibility, and
174	base-change-commutation. Those refinements live as separate instances /
175	follow-up lemmas in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the clopen
176	open-immersion conclusion as a Nonempty-witness.
177	
178	iter-186 body: the inclusion morphism is `Over.homMk (identityComponentCarrier G).ι`,
179	with the over-category compatibility holding by definition of
180	`IdentityComponent G`. The `.left` of this morphism is
181	`(identityComponentCarrier G).ι`, an open immersion by the global
182	`Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι` instance. For the closed-immersion
183	half we apply `IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion` to the open immersion
184	and reduce to `IsClosed (↑(identityComponentCarrier G) : Set _)`. The
185	remaining inline `sorry` is the substantive content: the carrier of
186	`identityComponentCarrier G` is closed, by "closure of a connected subspace
187	is connected" applied to the connected component through the image of the
188	identity section. -/
189	theorem IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
190	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
191	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
192	    Nonempty {f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G //
193	        IsOpenImmersion f.left ∧ IsClosedImmersion f.left} := by
194	  -- Over-category compatibility for the underlying open immersion.
195	  have hcomp : (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
196	    simp [IdentityComponent]
197	  -- The over-morphism witness.
198	  let f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G :=
199	    Over.homMk (U := IdentityComponent G) (V := G) (identityComponentCarrier G).ι hcomp
200	  refine ⟨⟨f, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
201	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` (definitionally; `Over.homMk_left` simp).
202	    -- An open immersion by `Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι`.
203	    change IsOpenImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
204	    infer_instance
205	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` is a preimmersion (open immersion ⟹
206	    -- immersion ⟹ preimmersion). Its range is the carrier set (`Scheme.Opens.range_ι`),
207	    -- and closure of that connected subset is itself (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3):
208	    -- the connected component through the identity is closed in `|G|`).
209	    change IsClosedImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
210	    apply IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion
211	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
212	    -- Substantive sorry: closedness of the (open) connected component of `|G|` through
213	    -- the identity section — the "closure of a connected subspace is connected"
214	    -- argument in Kleiman's proof of lem:agps~(3) paragraph 1.
215	    sorry
216	
217	end GroupScheme

220	
221	We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
222	`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
223	integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).
224	
225	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.

228	namespace Scheme
229	
230	/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.
231	
232	Defined as the identity component
233	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
234	`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
235	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
236	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
237	open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
238	geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
239	base field.
240	
241	iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
242	once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
243	`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
244	locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
245	`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
246	noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
247	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
248	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
249	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
250	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
251	  sorry
252	
253	/-! ## §3. The degree map
254	
255	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
256	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
257	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
258	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
259	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
260	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
261	
262	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
263	
264	namespace PicScheme
265	
266	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
267	
268	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
269	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
270	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
271	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
272	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
273	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
274	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
275	relative Picard functor).
276	
277	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
278	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
279	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
280	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
281	
282	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
283	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
284	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
285	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
286	`sorry`. -/
287	noncomputable def degree {k : Type u} [Field k]
288	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
289	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
290	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
291	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
292	  sorry
293	
294	end PicScheme
295	
296	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
297	
298	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
299	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
300	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
301	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
302	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
303	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
304	
305	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
306	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
307	
308	namespace Pic0Scheme
309	
310	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
311	
312	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
313	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
314	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
315	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
316	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
317	
318	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
319	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
320	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
321	
322	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
323	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
324	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
325	follow-up.
326	
327	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
328	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
329	  `PicScheme C`),
330	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
331	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
332	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
333	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
334	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
335	  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
336	  identity component).
337	For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
338	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
339	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
340	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
341	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
342	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
343	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
344	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
345	  sorry
346	
347	end Pic0Scheme
348	
349	end Scheme

122	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
123	
124	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
125	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
126	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
127	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
128	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
129	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
130	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
131	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
132	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
133	
134	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
135	01HH). -/
136	
137	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
138	curve. Sends an `O_T`-module `N` to the `O_(C ×_S T)`-module
139	`π_T^* N`, induced by the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on
140	`Scheme.Modules`.
141	
142	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the body is the
143	direct application of Mathlib's `Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)`
144	functor at `N`. The invertibility-preservation step (Stacks 01HH) is
145	deferred to iter-187+ once `OnProduct` carries an invertibility witness. -/
146	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
147	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (N : T.Modules) : OnProduct πC πT :=
148	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N
149	
150	/-! ## §3. Composition of pullbacks
151	
152	The composition law for line-bundle pullback (Stacks tag 01HG) says that for any
153	composable morphisms `f : X ⟶ Y, g : Y ⟶ Z` and any line bundle `M ∈ Pic(Z)`,
154	the natural isomorphism `(g ∘ f)^* M ≅ f^* g^* M` makes the pullback functor
155	into a (pseudo-)functor `Sch^op ⥤ Cat`.
156	
157	Specialised to the relative curve setting: given `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the
158	base-change morphism `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` satisfies
159	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` (definition of the fiber product). Therefore on
160	line bundles `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*`, with the equality realised as the
161	canonical natural isomorphism of pullback functors via the pseudo-functoriality
162	of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (Mathlib `Modules.pullbackComp`).
163	
164	Blueprint reference: `lem:pullback_compose` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
165	
166	/-- **Composition of line-bundle pullbacks** along the projection. For a base
167	scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects `T, T'` over `S`
168	via `πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
169	(encoded by the hypothesis `πT' = g ≫ πT`), set
170	`g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T`
171	(the base-change morphism, given by `pullback.map`). On the underlying
172	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` functors the two routes through the canonical
173	naturality square give canonically isomorphic objects:
174	```
175	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)
176	  T.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T).Modules
177	       │                                                              │
178	       │ Scheme.Modules.pullback g            Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C
179	       ▼                                                              ▼
180	 T'.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T').Modules
181	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')
182	```
183	i.e. for any `N : T.Modules`,
184	```
185	(Modules.pullback g_C).obj ((Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N)
186	  ≅
187	(Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')).obj ((Modules.pullback g).obj N).
188	```

55	\section{Line bundles on a relative curve}
56	\label{sec:lbp_line_bundle}
57	
58	\begin{definition}\leanok
59	[Line bundle on a product]
60	  \label{def:line_bundle_on_product}
61	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.OnProduct}
62	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", §2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
63	  % Definition df:aPf (absolute Picard functor)
64	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1279).
65	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
66	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
67	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
68	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
69	  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)"
70	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', §2,
71	  Def.~``absolute Picard functor''
72	  (cf.\ Stacks tag 01HK, Hartshorne II~§6).}
73	  Let \(k\) be a base field, \(C\) a \(k\)-scheme (in our application, a smooth proper
74	  geometrically integral curve), and \(T\) a \(k\)-scheme. A
75	  \emph{line bundle on the relative curve \(C \times_k T\)} is an invertible
76	  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)-module, that is, an
77	  \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)-module \(\mathcal{L}\) such that there exists an
78	  open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(C \times_k T\) on which each restriction
79	  \(\mathcal{L}|_{U_i}\) is free of rank one. Two line bundles are isomorphic if
80	  they are isomorphic as \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)-modules. The set of
81	  isomorphism classes forms an abelian group under tensor product (the
82	  \emph{Picard group}), denoted
83	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\). In Kleiman's notation with \(X = C\) and \(S = \Spec k\),
84	  this is exactly the value
85	  \(\Pic_C(T) = \Pic(C \times_k T)\) of the absolute Picard functor.
86	\end{definition}
87	
88	% NOTE: iter-174 Lane E (file-skeleton) encodes the carrier `OnProduct πC πT`
89	% as `Type (u+1) := sorry` (a TYPE-level scaffold sorry), mirroring the
90	% iter-173 precedent for `Scheme.QcohAlgebra`. Mathlib commit `b80f227`

99	\section{The pullback functor along the projection}
100	\label{sec:lbp_pullback_projection}
101	
102	If \(f : Y \to Z\) is a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{M}\) is an invertible
103	\(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-module, the pullback \(f^*\mathcal{M}\) is an invertible
104	\(\mathcal{O}_Y\)-module (the pullback of an open cover trivialising \(\mathcal{M}\)
105	trivialises \(f^*\mathcal{M}\), and the pullback of a free module of rank one is
106	free of rank one). This is a standard preservation fact (Stacks tag 01HH); we
107	specialise it to the projection \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) and the
108	\(\mathcal{O}_T\)-Picard group.
109	
110	\begin{definition}\leanok
111	[Pullback along the projection]
112	  \label{def:pullback_along_projection}
113	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection}
114	  
115	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", §2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
116	  % Definition df:aPf (functoriality of the absolute Picard functor)
117	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1290).
118	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
119	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
120	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
121	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
122	  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)
123	  %  [\dots]
124	  %  The absolute Picard functor  is a ``prepared presheaf'' in this
125	  % sense: given any family of  \(S\)-schemes \(T_i\), we have
126	  %     \(\)\Pic_X\bigl(\textstyle\coprod T_i\bigr)=\prod\Pic_X(T_i).\(\)"
127	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', §2,
128	  Def.~``absolute Picard functor'' and the ``prepared presheaf'' paragraph
129	  (cf.\ Stacks tag 01HH).}
130	  Let \(C/k\) and \(T/k\) be as in
131	  \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product}, and let \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\)
132	  denote the canonical projection. The \emph{pullback along \(\pi_T\)} is the
133	  group homomorphism (in fact, the set map; the additive structure is not used
134	  in this chapter)
135	  \[
136	    \pi_T^* \;\colon\; \Pic(T) \;\longrightarrow\; \Pic(C \times_k T),
137	    \qquad [\mathcal{N}] \;\longmapsto\; [\pi_T^* \mathcal{N}],
138	  \]

238	\section{The relative Picard presheaf (set-valued)}
239	\label{sec:lbp_relpic_quotient}
240	
241	We now package the pullback functor into the relative Picard presheaf. As
242	explained in the planner note at the top of the chapter, we work with the
243	Set-valued version; the group refinement is deferred to A.1.c.
244	
245	\begin{theorem}\leanok
246	[The relative Picard quotient is well defined]
247	  \label{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
248	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup}
249	  
250	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", §2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
251	  % Definition df:Pfs (relative Picard functor)
252	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1311-L1318).
253	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
254	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
255	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
256	  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)
257	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
258	  % topologies  by
259	  %     \(\)\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
260	  %     \Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.\(\)
261	  %  \end{dfn}"
262	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', §2,
263	  Def.~``relative Picard functor'' df:Pfs.}
264	  Let \(C/k\) be as in \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product} and let \(T\) be a
265	  \(k\)-scheme. The subset
266	  \[
267	    H_T \;\;:=\;\; \pi_T^* \Pic(T) \;\;\subseteq\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T)
268	  \]
269	  is the image of the pullback map \(\pi_T^*\)
270	  (\cref{def:pullback_along_projection}); in particular it contains the trivial
271	  class and is closed under the multiplication of \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) (so it is
272	  a subgroup). Define
273	  \[
274	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, H_T,
275	  \]
276	  the quotient set whose elements are equivalence classes \([\mathcal{L}]\) of
277	  line bundles \(\mathcal{L} \in \Pic(C \times_k T)\) under the relation
278	  \(\mathcal{L} \sim \mathcal{L}'\) iff
279	  \(\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{L}'^{-1} \in H_T\).
280	  Then \(T \mapsto \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a well-defined assignment from
281	  \(k\)-schemes to sets, and the natural map
282	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a surjection of sets.
283	\end{theorem}
284	
285	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: Kleiman §2 does not supply a separate "proof" block for
286	% df:Pfs --- the definition's well-definedness is a direct group-theoretic
287	% observation. The verbatim statement above is the full source content. The
288	% project-side proof is therefore a structural restatement in Lean syntax,
289	% not a translation of an external argument.

input.new_string:
### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)

- **`@[reducible] noncomputable def OnProduct := (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules` + iso-class setoid (`Nonempty (L ≅ L')`) is the iter-186 axiom-clean carrier-level skeleton for `LineBundle`/`RelPicPresheaf` Picard-pullback infrastructure when Mathlib lacks `IsInvertible`-on-`Scheme.Modules` predicate** *(NEW iter-186 Archon canonical — Lane A.1.b `Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean`, all 5 typed sorries Tier-1 axiom-clean.)* When the blueprint promises a Picard-quotient construction but Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `IsInvertible`-on-`Scheme.Modules` predicate (closest: `Module.Invertible` rings-only), drop the invertibility wrapper at the carrier level (substantive types, NOT type-weakening): `@[reducible] noncomputable def OnProduct := (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules`. The pullback functor `pullbackAlongProjection := (Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N` is a direct application; pseudo-functoriality via `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp + pullbackCongr + pullback.lift_snd` 3-step iso chain for `pullback_pullback_eq`. For the `RelPicPresheaf` set quotient, use an iso-class `Setoid` (`r L L' := Nonempty (L ≅ L')`) with `Iso.refl/symm/trans`-built `iseqv`. Trade-off: the full Pic quotient (with invertibility witness + π_T^* Pic(T) subgroup quotient via tensor + invertibility-inverse) is iter-187+ work — flag with `% NOTE (iter-NNN review)` annotations on the chapter blocks. Reusable for any Picard-style construction whose Mathlib substrate gap is identical (invertibility predicate absent at the pinned commit).

- **`RingHom.toAlgebra ∘ (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection ≫ Scheme.germToFunctionField)` is the 2-helper `Algebra kbar K(C)` bridge for geometrically-integral proper curves over algebraically-closed base** *(NEW iter-186 Archon canonical — Lane A OCofP `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instAlgebraKbarFunctionField` + `instNonemptyTopOpen`, axiom-clean.)* When a project-side declaration needs `Algebra kbar C.left.functionField` and the project lacks the instance, the canonical 2-helper bridge: (1) `instNonemptyTopOpen : Nonempty ((⊤ : C.left.Opens) : Scheme) := Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff.mpr ⟨Classical.choice ‹IsIntegral C.left›.nonempty, Set.mem_univ _⟩` (`IsIntegral C.left ⟹ Nonempty C.left`); (2) `instAlgebraKbarFunctionField : Algebra kbar K(C) := RingHom.toAlgebra (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection ≫ Scheme.germToFunctionField).toRingHom`. **Nonempty-synthesis trap**: `(⊤ : Opens X.toTopCat)` and `U : X.Opens` are sort-coercion-incompatible — resolve via the exact `C.left.Opens` spelling. Reusable for any project-side `Algebra k K(X)`-style bridge where `[GeometricallyIntegral X] + [IsAlgClosed k]` plus the global-sections-then-stalk composition gives the algebraMap.

- **`Abelian.Ext.postcompOfLinear` + (`comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero` + `mk₀_comp_mk₀` + `Iso.hom_inv_id` + `comp_mk₀_id`) is the explicit `LinearEquiv (HModule kbar F n) (HModule kbar G n)` recipe per-n for kbar-linear χ-invariance under sheaf iso** *(NEW iter-186 Archon canonical — Lane H `eulerCharacteristic_iso` in `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`, Tier-1 axiom-clean.)* For SES of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat kbar)` sheaves on a curve, χ-invariance under sheaf iso `F ≅ G` builds explicitly per degree: `LinearEquiv.ofLinear (Ext.postcompOfLinear (mk₀ e.hom) ⋯) (Ext.postcompOfLinear (mk₀ e.inv) ⋯) h₁ h₂`. Side conditions discharge by `change` to expose the underlying `Ext` composition then `comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero + mk₀_comp_mk₀ + Iso.hom_inv_id + comp_mk₀_id`. **Pitfall**: `h₁ : f.comp g = LinearMap.id` lives on N (the codomain); `h₂ : g.comp f = LinearMap.id` lives on M (the domain) — the two `?_` goals from `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` are NOT symmetric (got this wrong on first try → immediate type-mismatch). Reusable for any kbar-linear Ext-invariance proof under base sheaf iso.

- **`Submodule.mapQ` + `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` is the 25-LOC R⧸(x)-linear equivalence recipe `QuotSMulTop x R ≃ₗ[R⧸(x)] R⧸Ideal.span{x}`** *(NEW iter-186 Archon canonical — Lane G `regularLocal_inductive_step` R⧸(x) bridge in `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, axiom-clean.)* When the goal is to transport `IsRegular` from `R/I` to `QuotSMulTop x R` (or vice versa), build the `R⧸(x)`-linear equiv explicitly: first prove `heq : (x • ⊤ : Submodule R R) = Ideal.span {x}` via `Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists + Ideal.mem_span_singleton + Dvd.dvd` ext-membership; then `LinearEquiv.mk (Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.le) (Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.ge) ⟨refl, refl⟩ ⟨refl, refl⟩` with `map_smul'` reducing to `rfl` after `Quotient.inductionOn` on the scalar (the `R⧸(x)`-action on both modules is `[s] • [r] = [s * r]` definitionally). Transport via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. Reusable for any `IsRegular` transport between `QuotSMulTop x M` and `M ⧸ Ideal.span {x} • M` style quotients.

- **Privacy bypass via proof-irrelevance iso-chain reconstruction** *(NEW iter-185 Archon canonical — surfaced by Lane E `iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`.)*
